info.name: Acoustic Connect — GraphQL API (Live-Verified)
info.description:
Live-verified GraphQL operations for the Acoustic Connect API.

Endpoint: https://connect-gql-us-1.goacoustic.com

All requests require the `x-api-key` header. Set the `baseUrl` and `apiKey` collection variables before running.

⚠️ Schema vs Production: The R5 schema contains fields/mutations that do not exist on the live API. All requests in this collection reflect ACTUAL production behaviour, not the schema.

Verification status legend:
✅ LIVE-VERIFIED — tested with curl against production
🔵 DOCS-VERIFIED — confirmed from official Acoustic docs
⏳ PENDING — assumed from schema, not yet curl-tested

request.description: Returns API version and subscription ID. Useful for confirming connectivity.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

FIX: dataSets(type: AUDIENCE) → 'Unknown argument' error on production.
Correct syntax: dataSets(filter: [{ field: "type", eq: "AUDIENCE" }])

Change eq value to SEGMENT, PRODUCT_CATALOG etc. to query other types.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

Returns the product catalog DataSet. Use the returned dataSetId in product catalog mutations.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

FIXES:
- Correct casing is dataSetAttributeCategories (capital S)
- 'id' field does NOT exist on production — only name + icon are valid
- Schema shows 'datasetAttributeCategories' (lowercase s) — wrong on production

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

Returns a paginated list of contacts. Use endCursor + after: argument to paginate.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

FIX: There is NO singular contact(contactId) query on production.
Use contacts(filter: [{ and: [...] }]) instead.

Attribute names are CASE-SENSITIVE: 'Email' not 'email', 'Unique ID' not 'unique id'.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

FIX: Schema says response returns 'contactId' — production returns 'contactKey'.
Attribute names are CASE-SENSITIVE ('Email' not 'email').
dataSetId is optional — omit to use the default audience.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

Use 'key' (the unique ID attribute value) OR 'contactId' (UUID) — never both.
dataSetId is optional.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

Alternate form using contactId UUID instead of key. Use key OR contactId — not both.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

Use when your audience has a key attribute.
deleteReason options: RIGHT_TO_BE_FORGOTTEN, USER_REQUEST, ADMIN_ACTION

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

Use contact UUID (not the key value). Pass the actual UUID string, not the field name.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

Use when no key attribute is set. Field must be an addressable field (Email, Phone Number, etc.).
Field names are CASE-SENSITIVE.

🔵 DOCS-VERIFIED

FIXES:
- Must use GraphQL variables (not inline args)
- dataSetId is OPTIONAL
- fileLocation requires: { type: SFTP, filename, folder } — NOT key: path
- autoMap is not a valid field
- mappings use columnIndex + attributeName
- Returns { id } only — no status field in response

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

Monitor import jobs via status / startedAt / endedAt / errors.
Note: No row count fields available — only completion status and errors.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

FIX: 'key' field is in schema but does NOT exist on production.
Valid node fields: dataSetId + attributes { name value } only.

⏳ PENDING LIVE VERIFICATION

Response shape assumed from schema. Adjust mappings to match your key fields.

⏳ PENDING LIVE VERIFICATION

Response shape assumed from schema.

⏳ PENDING LIVE VERIFICATION

Fields assumed from schema. Verify field names against your production response.

⏳ PENDING LIVE VERIFICATION

Fields assumed from schema. Verify field names against your production response.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

Lists products from the catalog. Supports first, last, orderBy, selectByName, filter.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

FIX: filter must be a plain OBJECT — NOT an array.
Wrong: filter: [{ field: "productId", eq: "..." }]
Correct: filter: { field: "productId", eq: "..." }

🔵 DOCS-VERIFIED

productId is the key field and must be unique. Numeric values (unitPrice) should not be quoted.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

FIX: Correct signature is updateProduct(productId, updatedProduct) — NOT where/to.
Returns modifiedCount, NOT attributes.

✅ LIVE-VERIFIED ⚠️ IRREVERSIBLE

FIX: productIds is a TOP-LEVEL argument — NOT inside where: {}.
Wrong: deleteProducts(where: { productIds: [...] })
Correct: deleteProducts(productIds: [...])

item.name: Add Custom Attributes to Product Catalog ✅

request.description:
✅ LIVE-VERIFIED

FIX: createDataSetAttribute(... dataSetType: PRODUCT_CATALOG) does NOT work.
Correct approach: use updateDataSet with attributes.create.
Set catalogDataSetId from the 'Query DataSets — PRODUCT_CATALOG' request.

⏳ PENDING LIVE VERIFICATION

GDPR data export job. Response shape { jobId } assumed from schema.

⏳ PENDING LIVE VERIFICATION

⚠️ IRREVERSIBLE — permanently erases a contact from ALL Acoustic services.
Response shape { jobId } assumed from schema.
